title: Poppas Got A Brand New Badge

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, highly quotable, but not particularly original in this context

jokes.text: So hot. So sticky.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the heat wave
jokes.punchline: The overly descriptive, slightly uncomfortable way of describing the weather
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about heat, nothing particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Ah, the old folks. It happens every heat wave.
jokes.setup: Seeing elderly people in his store's freezer
jokes.punchline: Apu's casual acceptance of elderly people seeking relief in inappropriate places
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Apu's detached observation of human behavior, with mild dark humor

jokes.text: Okay, people, out of my freezer! But we're hot and elderly! I'm sorry, these are reserved for the recently deceased.
jokes.setup: Elderly people seeking relief in freezer
jokes.punchline: Freezer is reserved for dead bodies, not living elderly people
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about death and aging with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: This air conditioning is better than any truant officer. I'm seeing students I haven't seen in years.
jokes.setup: Students escaping heat by coming to school
jokes.punchline: Skinner's observation that air conditioning accomplishes what attendance enforcement couldn't
jokes.explanation: Clever observation that perfectly captures Skinner's educational administrator perspective

jokes.text: We're wasting more energy than Ricky Martin's girlfriend. Hey-oh!
jokes.setup: Commenting on energy waste during heat wave
jokes.punchline: Reference to Ricky Martin being gay, making his 'girlfriend' waste energy pretending
jokes.explanation: Dated celebrity reference joke that relies on gossip rather than clever construction

jokes.text: Gentlemen, our city's sucking down the juice like my wife at an open bar.
jokes.setup: Describing the city's energy consumption
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his wife's drinking problem at free events
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Quimby's inappropriate oversharing and colorful metaphors

jokes.text: We've siphoned off extra power from the orphanage. Who are they going to complain to? Their parents?
jokes.setup: Burns explaining how he got extra power
jokes.punchline: Orphans can't complain to parents they don't have
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns moment combining his callousness with perfect logical structure and dark humor

jokes.text: Lisa, if we start conserving, the environmentalists win!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer to conserve energy
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic treating conservation as losing to environmentalists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - turning environmental responsibility into a competition he refuses to lose

jokes.text: Every time Santa and I get together, it's a disaster.
jokes.setup: Blackout occurs when Homer plugs in Christmas decoration
jokes.punchline: Homer anthropomorphizing Santa as a problematic relationship partner
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating Santa as a real person Homer has relationship problems with

jokes.text: I heard tonight they have Ed Bradley versus Mister Ed.
jokes.setup: Missing Fox's Celebrity Boxing due to blackout
jokes.punchline: Absurd matchup between 60 Minutes journalist and talking horse based on name similarity
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining real person with fictional horse, good economy of language

jokes.text: I got some yuppie jerkoff headed right for me, yakking away on his cell phone. I hear that. I got some big shot barreling down on me. Hey! Who are you talking to? Your boyfriend? Hey, jackass! Your voice sounds familiar!
jokes.setup: Two drivers complaining about each other on the phone
jokes.punchline: They're talking to each other without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed ironic situation with good build-up and revelation

jokes.text: I don't hear an alarm. Let's take stuff. Whoa! Isn't that stealing? No, it's just looting. Sweet. Let's go nuts.
jokes.setup: Kids considering taking things during blackout
jokes.punchline: Distinction between 'stealing' and 'looting' makes it acceptable to Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing kids' flawed moral reasoning through semantic distinction

jokes.text: Mannequins. You can't buy these!
jokes.setup: Bart looting during the blackout
jokes.punchline: Excitement over stealing something that's literally for sale
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Bart's flawed understanding of what's valuable or restricted

jokes.text: I think what the viewers want to know, Arnie, is, is my house okay? You mean, is your giant castle okay, Kent? Don't hate me because I bought at the right time, Arnie.
jokes.setup: News coverage of city riots
jokes.punchline: Kent's narcissistic concern for his own property during citywide chaos
jokes.explanation: Good character moment highlighting Kent's selfishness and Arnie's resentment

jokes.text: Oh, no! They're stealing the tire fire!
jokes.setup: Witnessing looting during riots
jokes.punchline: Concern over theft of Springfield's environmental disaster/landmark
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - who would steal a tire fire? Perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: Finally, some repression.
jokes.setup: Police arriving to stop riots
jokes.punchline: Sideshow Bob's intellectual appreciation for authoritarian control
jokes.explanation: Perfect character line - Bob's intellectual vocabulary applied to wanting order restored

jokes.text: All right, everyone! Disperse immediately! We are prepared to use force! What, what? We're not prepared, Eddie?
jokes.setup: Wiggum making authoritative threat to crowd
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermined by admitting they're not actually prepared
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - good setup/deflation structure

jokes.text: Someone call 911. Oh, they never come.
jokes.setup: Emergency situation requiring police
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance that emergency services are unreliable
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about Springfield's dysfunctional services

jokes.text: Look at those looters breaking windows, setting fires! They're living my dream, and you won't let me join them!
jokes.setup: Homer watching rioters
jokes.punchline: His jealousy and desire to participate in destruction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's childish destructive impulses

jokes.text: Please, can I throw one little... No. What if I just burn down a... No. Can I at least incite further... No.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to join the rioting
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly specific riot activities being denied
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's specific riot fantasies and Marge's firm boundaries

jokes.text: Can I have a beer? All right. But not the imported. Homer? You've got to set limits, Marge. No beer!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for beer, Marge setting limits on imported beer
jokes.punchline: Marge completely reversing and denying all beer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Marge taking Homer's advice too far

jokes.text: Get your T-shirts here! 'I survived the Springfield riot!'
jokes.setup: Aftermath of riots
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy immediately monetizing the disaster with merchandise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Comic Book Guy's opportunistic capitalism

jokes.text: This be a superball.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain's missing glass eye
jokes.punchline: He's using a superball as replacement, bouncing in the socket
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual callback with absurd practical solution to previous joke

jokes.text: We called them men. I agree with the hideous crone! Yeah! Hear, hear! She's ugly!
jokes.setup: Grampa making speech about past having better men
jokes.punchline: Crowd agrees with his message while insulting him personally
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing crowd's mixed response - agreeing with message while insulting messenger

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Blame the victims.
jokes.setup: Being blamed for police incompetence during riots
jokes.punchline: Wiggum calling himself and police the 'victims' of the situation they failed to control
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's inability to accept responsibility

jokes.text: A committee? Did he say blue-ribbon? Committees don't get any better than that. Man, am I appeased.
jokes.setup: Mayor announcing formation of blue-ribbon committee
jokes.punchline: Citizens being easily satisfied by meaningless bureaucratic gestures
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how empty political gestures can pacify the public

jokes.text: Yeah. Wiggum couldn't catch cooties at Milhouse's birthday party.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Wiggum's police competence
jokes.punchline: Couldn't catch something that spreads easily at a kid's party
jokes.explanation: Good insult with specific, absurd scenario that emphasizes incompetence

jokes.text: Oh! Seriously, everyone says your parties rock.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally insulting Milhouse's parties
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to recover by complimenting the parties
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer social awkwardness moment with attempted recovery

jokes.text: I won't feel guilty until I can put a human face on this.
jokes.setup: Homer admitting he screwed up but doesn't feel guilty yet
jokes.punchline: Need to see actual human suffering before feeling bad
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's need for concrete consequences to feel empathy

jokes.text: Marge, doesn't Lisa have a human face? Yes, and she's crying.
jokes.setup: Homer needing to see human suffering
jokes.punchline: Lisa crying provides the exact human face Homer needed to see
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with perfect setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: They couldn't catch a cold with a cold-catching thing.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing police incompetence
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical completion of 'couldn't catch a cold' saying
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic struggle with completing common sayings

jokes.text: See, when you don't use Milhouse, it's hard. I love this kid.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to complete the 'cold-catching' insult
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging that using Milhouse as a reference point makes insults easier
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on Homer's own joke-making process

jokes.text: See, when you don't use Milhouse, it's hard. I love this kid.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with expression about catching cold
jokes.punchline: Realizing Milhouse makes insults easier, expressing affection for Bart
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's self-awareness about his insult patterns

jokes.text: Dad, that's Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting composite sketch of prime suspect
jokes.punchline: The sketch is just a drawing of Bart
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with perfect economy - Homer's 'detective work' is just blaming Bart

jokes.text: Look at him over there, eating that apple. What's he planning? What?
jokes.setup: Homer being suspicious of Bart
jokes.punchline: Finding sinister motives in ordinary apple-eating
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid Homer moment finding conspiracy in mundane activity

jokes.text: I think they demean women! Well, think again, son. You're going to juvie. But I just got out of juvie. Good! 'Cause I need directions.
jokes.setup: Homer arresting teen for stealing dolls, teen has noble motivation
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't care about motivation, just needs directions to juvenile hall
jokes.explanation: Good escalating joke showing Homer's single-minded approach to justice

jokes.text: I felt like a big man pushing that kid around!
jokes.setup: Homer describing his satisfaction with stopping crime
jokes.punchline: His pleasure comes from bullying rather than justice
jokes.explanation: Good dark character moment revealing Homer's less noble motivations

jokes.text: Cheese! That's muy picante!
jokes.setup: Homer throwing cheese at armed robber
jokes.punchline: Cheese is apparently spicy enough to stop criminal
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist action sequence - cheese as effective weapon

jokes.text: You know, I've had a lot of jobs. Boxer, mascot, astronaut, imitation Krusty, baby-proofer, trucker, hippie, plow driver, food critic, conceptual artist, grease salesman, carny, mayor, grifter, bodyguard for the mayor, country-western manager, garbage commissioner, mountain climber, farmer, inventor, Smithers, Poochie, celebrity assistant, power plant worker, fortune cookie writer, beer baron, Kwik-E-Mart clerk, homophobe and missionary.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on his career path
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long list of his previous jobs from the series
jokes.explanation: Great callback to series history, perfect character integration, though lacks economy

jokes.text: Finally, a way to combine my love of helping people with my love of hurting people!
jokes.setup: Homer considering security work
jokes.punchline: His motivation combines noble and violent impulses
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment perfectly capturing Homer's contradictory nature with great structure

jokes.text: You'll do as I say, or I'll have your badges! Once I make and give you your badges.
jokes.setup: Homer making authoritative threat to his recruits
jokes.punchline: Realizing he hasn't actually given them badges yet
jokes.explanation: Good setup/deflation showing Homer's premature authority assertion

jokes.text: A monster! Is this you? If it is, don't dial 911. Simply dial 636-555-3472.
jokes.setup: TV commercial for SpringShield security service
jokes.punchline: Instructions not to call real emergency services, call them instead
jokes.explanation: Good satire of private security advertising replacing public services

jokes.text: Friend? The only friend you need is SpringShield.
jokes.setup: Old lady asking about having a friend in the commercial
jokes.punchline: SpringShield replacing all human relationships
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about commercialization isolating people from real relationships

jokes.text: Monster put in wallet.
jokes.setup: Commercial showing what happens to the 'monster'
jokes.punchline: Monster is somehow stored in a wallet
jokes.explanation: Absurdist visual gag with good economy but unclear logic

jokes.text: Green vegetables and homework.
jokes.setup: Kid asking how Homer got big and strong
jokes.punchline: Homer giving completely false wholesome advice
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Homer giving advice contrary to his actual lifestyle

jokes.text: I didn't know my chin went that far up. Oh, yeah, that's the pride.
jokes.setup: Homer holding his head high with pride
jokes.punchline: Physical discovery about his own anatomy through pride posture
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Homer's self-discovery

jokes.text: Even dessert? Dessert is three desserts.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining all three courses are dessert
jokes.punchline: Even the dessert course is multiple desserts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd dinner concept with perfect economy

jokes.text: Behind these doors are the finest cops ever to wriggle into size-46 pants.
jokes.setup: Mayor praising police force
jokes.punchline: Unflattering description of police physical fitness
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment structure revealing rather than praising

jokes.text: Wiggum? Am I getting warmer?
jokes.setup: Mayor looking for police behind doors
jokes.punchline: Wiggum treating his own presence like a guessing game
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's childlike approach to adult situations

jokes.text: Oh, wait. It was better the other way. Police? Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating becoming Chief of Police
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing the responsibility and regretting it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's delayed realization of consequences

jokes.text: You can let me go, but I'll just keep exposing myself at the mall.
jokes.setup: Homer freeing the town drunk to cut overhead
jokes.punchline: Otis warning he'll continue his inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard character moment with mild shock value

jokes.text: What a character.
jokes.setup: Otis threatening to expose himself
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inappropriate casual response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Homer's obliviousness to serious issues

jokes.text: Dad, make him stop! Well, according to this, he's telling the truth.
jokes.setup: Lisa being insulted by Bart using lie detector
jokes.punchline: Homer siding with the machine rather than defending Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Homer's misplaced faith in technology over family

jokes.text: One of these ferrets is wearing a wire.
jokes.setup: Homer finding Fat Tony's operation
jokes.punchline: Using a ferret as an undercover recording device
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist concept - ferret as undercover agent with great originality

jokes.text: You're not a pet and you're not a friend. You're nothing to me.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony discovering the wire-wearing ferret
jokes.punchline: Rejecting the ferret with mob-style coldness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment applying mob betrayal rhetoric to a ferret

jokes.text: Daddy, how come you're not at work? I don't know. How come you're not at school? My teacher says she's tired of trying. Yeah, well, so am I, Ralphie. So am I.
jokes.setup: Ralph asking why Wiggum isn't at work
jokes.punchline: Both father and son have given up on their respective responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure showing family dysfunction and mutual giving up

jokes.text: Last year, I was Jar Jar Binks.
jokes.setup: Homer recounting his trick-or-treating history to gain support
jokes.punchline: Admitting to dressing as universally hated character
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that relies on audience knowledge of character's unpopularity

jokes.text: Oh! Well, I'd love to help you, Homer, but I have too darn much to live for. I just discovered Thai food.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for help fighting Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: Hibbert's excuse is discovering ethnic cuisine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with appropriately trivial excuse for cowardice

jokes.text: I'd help you, but I have yet to kiss a human girl.
jokes.setup: Homer seeking help against mobsters
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's romantic inexperience as excuse
jokes.explanation: Standard Comic Book Guy self-deprecation with good character integration

jokes.text: And I've got a TiVo full of unwatched Dharma and Gregs.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy explaining why he can't risk his life
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing mediocre TV show over heroism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Comic Book Guy's media priorities over real life

jokes.text: Sorry, Homer. I'm a coward now, like all recovering alcoholics.
jokes.setup: Barney refusing to help Homer fight
jokes.punchline: Claiming cowardice is part of recovery process
jokes.explanation: Good character development joke connecting sobriety to risk aversion

jokes.text: In one car? With no air conditioning? And the little poop machine going every 20 minutes?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting family could leave town together
jokes.punchline: Homer's escalating complaints about traveling with baby Maggie
jokes.explanation: Good escalating complaints showing Homer's selfishness even in crisis

jokes.text: I ain't sayin' nothin'. Who says I have a mother?
jokes.setup: Fat Tony asking Johnny Tightlips basic questions
jokes.punchline: Tightlips treating every question as potential incrimination
jokes.explanation: Excellent character concept - mobster so paranoid he won't admit basic facts

jokes.text: I mean, you could be a pizza man, organ grinder, leaning-tower maker, and did I say pizza man?
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing Italian-American stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Her alternative suggestions are equally stereotypical
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Marge's unconscious perpetuation of stereotypes while criticizing them

jokes.text: You are listing my broken dreams.
jokes.setup: Marge listing alternative careers for Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: Those were actually his abandoned aspirations
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment giving unexpected depth to Fat Tony with perfect economy

jokes.text: I finally did a job where I wasn't lazy, stupid, or corrupt and now I'm going to get killed for it.
jokes.setup: Homer facing death for his police work
jokes.punchline: Recognizing this is his first competent job performance
jokes.explanation: Great character self-awareness about his work history and current irony

jokes.text: Yeah. You can kill me, but someone will take my place. And if you kill him, someone will take his place. And that's pretty much the end of it. The town will be yours.
jokes.setup: Homer making defiant last stand speech
jokes.punchline: Accidentally conceding the town will fall after two deaths
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of heroic speech trope with perfect Homer logic failure

jokes.text: I see a lot of things. You know, you could be a little more helpful.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Tightlips if he can see the shooter
jokes.punchline: Tightlips' non-answer maintains his character while being useless
jokes.explanation: Good callback to character trait with practical frustration

jokes.text: That's funny. Because this is how I got the job the first time.
jokes.setup: Homer giving badge to next person he sees
jokes.punchline: Wiggum revealing this is how he originally became police chief
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation explaining Springfield's systemic incompetence

jokes.text: Hell, they would've taken my squad car if I hadn't hid it under some hay.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining what was taken from him
jokes.punchline: Hiding police car with completely inadequate camouflage
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist visual of police car poorly hidden under hay

jokes.text: She's just like Clark Kent. When there's lots of excitement, she's nowhere to be found.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Maggie's absence during action
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to Clark Kent without realizing the superhero implication
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic comparison - Homer doesn't realize he's describing superhero behavior
